description: PyTorch op or Python construct not supported by dynamo
name: Unsupported Operation

description: Branching on tensor values (e.g., if x.item() > 0)
name: Data-Dependent Control Flow

description: Shape expressions too complex for symbolic reasoning
name: Dynamic Shapes Complexity

description: In-place ops that violate functional semantics
name: In-Place Mutation

description: Custom tensor subclasses with non-standard behavior
name: Tensor Subclass

description: User-defined torch.autograd.Function not traceable
name: Custom Autograd Function

description: Calls to libraries dynamo cannot trace into
name: Third-Party Library Call

description: Input doesn't match cached guards — triggers recompile
name: Guard Mismatch

description: Bug in inductor, triton, or other compilation backend
name: Backend Bug

description: Op lacks a decomposition into supported primitives
name: Missing Decomposition

description: Compile conflicts with DDP, FSDP, or tensor parallel
name: Distributed Interaction

description: Custom op not registered via torch.library or missing FakeTensor/meta kernel
name: Non-Compliant Custom Operator

description: Default triton config heuristics (tile sizes, num_warps) produce suboptimal kernel performance
name: Suboptimal Inductor Heuristics

description: Debug logging (e.g., everstore uploads, graph dumps) causing compile time bloat
name: Excessive Logging/Debug Overhead

description: Custom op libraries (flash attention, xformers) not ABI-compatible with PyTorch version
name: ABI Incompatibility

description: Dynamo's automatic shape dynamism causing unexpected recompilation or suboptimal code
name: Automatic Dynamic Shapes

description: Input shapes change across iterations, triggering automatic_dynamic_shapes recompilation
name: shape_wobble

description: Int members on nn.Module change values (e.g., step count) causing recompilation without allow_unspec_int_on_nn_module
name: unspecialized_int

description: Ops like nonzero/unique have data-dependent output shapes, causing graph break without capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops
name: dynamic_output_shape

description: Operations return scalar types (e.g., .item()) which cause graph break without capture_scalar_outputs
name: scalar_return

description: Custom compile wrappers that jam config options cause silent graph breaks (anti-pattern)
name: fancy_compile_wrapper

description: Root cause of the cache miss during AC recompute — Dynamo appears to be mutating the device_mesh attribute on user code.
name: Dynamo mutating device_mesh attribute

provenance.evidence: Could someone help investigate why Dynamo seems to be mutating the user code's device_mesh attribute?

description: Overguarding on frames with no graph output may cause unnecessary recompilations, e.g. when optimized bytecode is identical across invocations.
name: overguarding causing spurious recompiles

provenance.evidence: maybe we are overguarding in such cases

description: The TorchScriptObject (TorchComm object) is identified as the entity causing the graph break on object.__getattribute__.
name: TorchScriptObject triggering graph break
